name: engram
description: Persistent user-level memory shared across AI agents. Plain-markdown store the agent recalls at session start and distills into after work — memory that stays.

hooks.command: python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/session_start.py" 2>/dev/null || python "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/session_start.py" 2>/dev/null || py -3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/session_start.py" 2>/dev/null || echo "Engram (degraded mode): Python 3 was not found, so tooling is offline. Your persistent memory still works: read MEMORY.md in ~/.agent-memory (or %USERPROFILE%\.agent-memory) and open relevant memory files directly; follow the conventions shown in any existing file. Offer the user a consent-gated Python 3 install (winget install Python.Python.3.12 / sudo apt install python3 / brew install python3) to restore full tooling. Never install without consent."
